# EMS ProtoQuiz

> EMS ProtoQuiz turns your EMS agency's own protocol PDF into pharmacology quizzes, algorithm tests, and adaptive patient scenarios. Every answer is page-cited back to the source PDF, so you study YOUR local protocols — not generic NREMT filler. Free iOS app, built by a paramedic. Made by ProtoQuiz (Teach Me to Live LLC).

## What it is

- A study app for EMTs and paramedics that quizzes you on your own agency, county, or state protocols.
- You upload a protocol PDF (formulary, treatment protocols, algorithms, procedures). ProtoQuiz extracts it into quiz content.
- Quiz types: pharmacology (adult/pediatric doses, indications, contraindications, routes, mechanism, adverse effects), algorithm tests from your flowcharts, adaptive patient scenarios where the patient responds to your treatment decisions, and Learn Mode (spaced repetition on the meds you miss).
- Every answer links to the exact source page in your uploaded PDF. Nothing is generated outside that document; you can edit any extraction.
- Free and ad-supported. Optional paid upgrade removes ads. No features are paywalled.
- iOS only today; Android is in development.

## Who it is for

- Paramedic and EMT students studying their local protocols.
- New providers, or providers moving to a new agency/county/state who must learn an unfamiliar protocol set.
- Seasoned providers honing mastery of their current system.
- EMS agencies and training officers who want a protocol-study tool for their staff (B2B).

## Key links

- Home: https://protoquiz.com/
- iOS App Store: https://apps.apple.com/app/id6753611139
- For agencies (B2B): https://protoquiz.com/agency/
- Blog (EMS study guides + product updates): https://protoquiz.com/blog/
- Privacy: https://protoquiz.com/privacy/
- Terms: https://protoquiz.com/terms/

## Facts

- Rating: 4.7 stars on the App Store.
- Platform: iOS (iPhone/iPad). Android planned, not yet released.
- Maker: ProtoQuiz, by Teach Me to Live LLC, founded 2025.
- Differentiator vs. NREMT-prep apps (e.g. Pocket Prep): ProtoQuiz quizzes YOUR local/agency protocols from your own PDF with page-cited answers, rather than a fixed bank of generic national-registry questions.
